free_text: -Fragment properties in the fission of Np-237 with
fast neutrons- An experimental investigation of
Pulsed proton beam on Li-7 for the 800. keV .
2.5 MeV deuterons on deuterium for the 5.5 MeV
data. Target was 2.6 mg/cm**2 thick composed of
-NpO2 sample placed 15 mm from neutron source, at
45 deg relative to the proton beam. Sample size
is 10 mm by 14 mm with thickness of 100 micro-g/cm**2.
Time of flight of the fission fragments with a
longest path length of 37.5 cm (and corresponding
Liquid scintillation detector with a 4.5 m path
and resolution less than 1 nsec (NE 213), energy
Li-6 glass detector of 5 mm thickness, 1 nsec
resolution for 1 m path. Energy resolution of 50 keV
at 800 keV. Used to measure neutron energies.
1000 mm**2 surface, 0.1 mm thick of P-type silicon
and N-type insulating layer with a resolution of
1 to 2 %  for the kinetic energy of the fission
fragments and a time resolution of less than 200 psec.
Other results for Np-237 in Subents
M.M. BIB and COMMON information was added.
Free text was corrected to be "human-readable".
-For energy loss of the fragments in the sample and
kinetic energy as a function of the fragment mass.
M.M. "AKE" was changed into "KE" in SF6 of
kinetic energy as a function of fragment mass.
